source_data.all_trainers.md:
## Champion
### LANCE
- Lv44 Gyarados: Flail, Rain Dance, Surf, Hyper Beam
- Lv47 Dragonite: Thunder Wave, Twister, Thunder, Hyper Beam
- Lv47 Dragonite: Thunder Wave, Twister, Blizzard, Hyper Beam
- Lv46 Aerodactyl: Wing Attack, AncientPower, Rock Slide, Hyper Beam
- Lv46 Charizard: Flamethrower, Wing Attack, Slash, Hyper Beam
- Lv50 Dragonite: Fire Blast, Safeguard, Outrage, Hyper Beam
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 24. Mt. Silver — Final Battle
### Silver Cave Summit — Trainer Red
- Trigger: Climb to summit
- Cutscene: Red says nothing ('...')
- Battle: Pikachu Lv81, Espeon Lv73, Snorlax Lv75, Venusaur Lv77, Charizard Lv77, Blastoise Lv77
- After: Credits roll again — true ending
- Red's team is the highest level in the game

## 16. Goldenrod Radio Tower Crisis
### Goldenrod Underground Warehouse — Executives
- Battles: Executive Proton, Executive Ariana, Executive Archer
- Story: Archer is the final boss, claims to carry on Giovanni's legacy
- After defeating Archer: Director rescued, Radio Tower liberated
### Radio Tower 5F — Reward
- Trigger: After clearing all Rockets
- Reward: CLEAR_BELL (Crystal) / RAINBOW_WING (Gold) / SILVER_WING (Silver)
- Flags set: EVENT_CLEARED_RADIO_TOWER
- Enables: Tin Tower access for legendary encounter (Crystal)

## 15. Lake of Rage + Mahogany — Badge 7
### Team Rocket Base (Mahogany) B1F-B3F
- Battles: ~10 Rocket Grunts, 3 Electrode Lv23 (switches), Executives
- Puzzle: Password doors ('HAIL GIOVANNI', spoken by Murkrow), Electrode power switches
- Story: Shut down signal forcing Magikarp evolution
- Lance assists: Heals your Pokemon during infiltration
- Reward: HM06 Whirlpool (from Lance)
- Flags set: EVENT_CLEARED_ROCKET_HIDEOUT
source_data.game_progression.md:
### Mt. Silver Entrance
- Trigger: All 16 badges required
- Route 28 gate opens
### Silver Cave Summit — Trainer Red
- Trigger: Climb to summit
- After: Credits roll again — true ending

unstructured_notes: Red's team is the only non-gym trainer battle that triggers credits. His Pikachu at Lv81 is the highest-level Pokemon in the game and unique because it uses Light Ball for power boost. The no-dialogue encounter is intentional — only '...' appears in the text box. The QA sprint should confirm that the 16-badge gate (all 8 Johto + all 8 Kanto) is checked at the Route 28 border gate, not just at Mt. Silver entrance. Team Rocket executive Proton appears at both Slowpoke Well (early game) and is referenced again in Goldenrod; ensure distinct trainer ID slots are used. Archer's defeat must set the flag that enables Clear Bell acquisition from the Radio Tower Director.

Implement Raikou data (Lv40, catch rate 3, moves: Roar/ThunderShock/Reflect/Crunch) and Entei data (Lv40, catch rate 3, moves: Roar/Fire Spin/Stomp/Flamethrower)

Implement Suicune's 6-encounter chain: flags EVENT_SAW_SUICUNE_AT_[LOCATION] at Burned Tower B1F, Cianwood City, Route 42, Vermilion City, Route 14, then final static battle at Tin Tower B1F (Lv40, moves: Rain Dance/Gust/Aurora Beam/Mist)
Implement Ho-Oh static encounter: Tin Tower 7F, Lv60, catch rate 3, requires Rainbow Wing + Clear Bell, moves: Sacred Fire/Safeguard/Gust/Recover
Implement Lugia static encounter: Whirl Islands B2F, Lv60, catch rate 3, requires Silver Wing, moves: Aeroblast/Safeguard/Gust/Recover
Implement InitRoamMons routine: called when Burned Tower B1F beast release cutscene completes, initializes roaming mon route data and HP tracking

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Suicune (Crystal Storyline)
Location: Multiple encounters, final battle at Tin Tower
Level: 40
In Pokemon Crystal, Suicune has a unique storyline. You encounter it at:
1. Burned Tower (flees, alongside Raikou and Entei)
2. Cianwood City (cutscene, flees)
3. Route 42 (cutscene, flees)
4. Vermilion City (cutscene, flees)
5. Route 14 (cutscene, flees)
6. Tin Tower B1F (static battle, Lv40) — requires Clear Bell + all 3 Legendary Beasts seen
Moves: Rain Dance, Gust, Aurora Beam, Mist
Catch Rate: 3

### Raikou (Roaming)
Level: 40
Mechanics: Appears randomly in Johto grass areas. Flees immediately on encounter. HP and status persist between encounters.
- Changes route every time you change routes
- Cannot encounter it on water or in caves
- If KOed, it is gone forever (no respawn)
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Roar, Thunder Shock, Reflect, Crunch

### Entei (Roaming)
Level: 40
Mechanics: Same roaming mechanics as Raikou.
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Roar, Fire Spin, Stomp, Flamethrower

### Lugia
Location: Whirl Islands B2F
Level: 60
Requirements: Silver Wing
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Aeroblast, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

### Ho-Oh
Location: Tin Tower 7F
Level: 60
Requirements: Rainbow Wing + Clear Bell
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Sacred Fire, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

## Roaming Mechanics
- Both released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor
- They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only)
- Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route
- If you enter their current route, there's a chance to encounter them in grass
- They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too)
- HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters
- If you KO either one, they disappear permanently
- Using Fly/Teleport to a route they're on works — they don't move when you Fly
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
### Burned Tower B1F — Release the Beasts
- Trigger: Fall through floor to B1F (coord_event), SCENE_BURNEDTOWERB1F_RELEASE_THE_BEASTS
- Cutscene: Raikou, Entei, Suicune awaken and flee one by one
  - Raikou runs left, Entei runs right, Suicune pauses to look at player then runs
- Flags set: EVENT_RELEASED_THE_BEASTS
- Enables: Raikou and Entei begin roaming Johto (special InitRoamMons)
- Enables: Suicune appears at Cianwood City (Crystal)
### Suicune Chase Sequence (Crystal-exclusive)
1. Burned Tower B1F (Ecruteak) — flees with beasts
2. Cianwood City (north rocks) — Eusine present, flees
3. Route 42 — flees
4. Vermilion City — flees
5. Route 14 — flees
6. Tin Tower (with Clear Bell) — final battle Lv40

unstructured_notes: Roaming Pokemon use a special data structure tracking their current map group/map number, remaining HP, status byte, and caught flag. When the player transitions between overworld maps that count as 'routes' (not cities/caves), the game calls UpdateRoamMons to randomly relocate each roaming beast to an adjacent valid route. The roaming encounter check happens during the normal grass encounter roll — if the player's current route matches the roamer's route, there's a secondary check to potentially trigger the roamer instead of a normal wild Pokemon. Suicune's cutscene encounters (not the final battle) are map script events, not battle triggers — they use sprite movement commands and flee without entering battle mode. The Eusine character appears at the Cianwood encounter but has his own trainer battle later in Dragon's Den.

goal: Implement remaining special encounters — Celebi (GS Ball), Lapras (Union Cave Friday), Red Gyarados, Sudowoodo, Snorlax
abstract_tasks: Implement Celebi GS Ball event: Kurt holds GS Ball for one in-game day, player places it at Ilex Forest shrine, Celebi appears at Lv30 (catch rate 45, moves: Leech Seed/Recover/Heal Bell/Safeguard)
Implement Red Gyarados static encounter: Lake of Rage center surf tile, Lv30, guaranteed shiny (all DVs 1/0/14/14/14/14 for shiny check), gives Red Scale on catch, triggers Lance meeting on shore
Implement Sudowoodo static encounter: Route 36 blocking tile, Lv20 Rock-type, triggered by SquirtBottle use from bag, one-time encounter
Implement Snorlax static encounter: Route 11 Kanto, Lv50, triggered by PokeFlute channel on radio (requires EXPN Card from Lavender after Power Plant quest), one-time encounter
Implement Lapras weekly encounter: Union Cave B2F water tile, available only on Fridays, Lv20, respawns each Friday
Implement event engine coordination for all special encounters: correct map script triggers, event flags set on encounter initiation, cannot re-trigger after catch or KO

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Celebi (GS Ball Event)
Location: Ilex Forest Shrine
Level: 30
Requirements: GS Ball (originally Japan-only mobile event)
Mechanics: Take GS Ball to Kurt in Azalea Town. After one day, he returns it. Place it on the shrine in Ilex Forest. Celebi appears.
Catch Rate: 45
Moves: Leech Seed, Recover, Heal Bell, Safeguard

### Red Gyarados
Location: Lake of Rage
Level: 30
Type: Water/Flying
Note: Always Shiny (guaranteed). Static encounter — battle or flee, but it's unique. Gives Red Scale when caught (trade to Mr. Pokemon for Exp. Share).

### Sudowoodo
Location: Route 36 (blocking the path)
Level: 20
Requirements: SquirtBottle from Goldenrod flower shop (need Whitney badge)
Note: One per save file. Use SquirtBottle to make it attack.

### Snorlax
Location: Route 11 (Kanto, blocking Diglett's Cave entrance)
Level: 50
Requirements: PokeFlute channel on the radio (from Lavender Tower upgrade)
Note: One per save file. Tune the radio to the PokeFlute channel while standing next to it.

### Odd Egg (Crystal Exclusive)
Location: Pokemon Communication Center
Level: 5 (when hatched)
Contains one of: Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Tyrogue, Smoochum, Elekid, Magby
Special: 14% chance of being Shiny
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
### Lake of Rage — Red Gyarados
- Trigger: Surf to center of lake
- Battle: Shiny Red Gyarados Lv30 (static encounter, guaranteed shiny)
- After: Meet Lance on shore, he's investigating Team Rocket signal
- Item: Red Scale (from Gyarados; trade to Mr. Pokemon for EXP Share)

### Lavender Radio Tower — EXPN Card
- Item: EXPN CARD (enables Kanto radio stations)
- Enables: Poke Flute channel (wake Snorlax on Routes 12 and 16)

unstructured_notes: Lapras is unique among these encounters as a weekly respawn (every Friday) rather than a truly one-time encounter. The game checks the day-of-week flag for Lapras's Union Cave tile. Red Gyarados's guaranteed shiny requires hardcoded DVs: pokecrystal sets DVs to 1/0/14/14/14/14 (Attack DV 1 produces female gender and shiny when Special DV is 10+). The Odd Egg at the Pokemon Communication Center (Crystal-only building replacing Goldenrod Pokemon Center 2F) has a 14% shiny chance encoded as a special hatching flag. Snorlax actually blocks the path to Diglett's Cave (Route 11) and is NOT on Routes 12/16 in Crystal — the Radio Tower in Lavender enables the PokeFlute frequency on the Pokegear radio, and the player must stand next to the Snorlax and open the radio to trigger the encounter.

goal: QA audit of sprints 160-161 — verify roaming mechanics (HP persists, route changing), Suicune triggers, legendary levels and movesets
abstract_tasks: Simulate Raikou and Entei roaming: verify HP and status persist when player re-encounters after switching routes multiple times
Verify roaming beast route-change logic: each overworld map transition triggers route reassignment, but Fly destinations do not cause route change
Verify all 6 Suicune encounter flags fire in correct sequence: no flag skip allowed, Tin Tower battle blocked if any prior encounter flag unset
Verify Red Gyarados DVs produce guaranteed shiny (Attack DV 1, Special DV >= 10), Red Scale item added to bag on catch
Verify Celebi encounter gating: GS Ball must have been given to Kurt, one real-time day elapsed (or in-game time equivalent), shrine placement triggers battle
Verify Lapras respawns on Friday without requiring any additional event flags, and does not respawn on other days
Confirm that KOing a roaming beast permanently removes it (flag set, no respawn on game reload), while Celebi, Sudowoodo, Snorlax also do not respawn after KO

source_data.special_encounters.md:
## Roaming Mechanics (Raikou & Entei)
- Both are released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor
- They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only)
- Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route
- If you enter their current route, there's a chance to encounter them in grass
- They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too)
- HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters
- If you KO either one, they disappear permanently
- Using Fly/Teleport to a route they're on works — they don't move when you Fly
- The Pokedex map feature shows their current location after first sighting

### Suicune Chase Sequence
1. Burned Tower B1F (Ecruteak) — flees with beasts
2. Cianwood City (north rocks) — Eusine present, flees
3. Route 42 — flees
4. Vermilion City — flees
5. Route 14 — flees
6. Tin Tower B1F (static battle, Lv40)
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
Each sighting sets a flag: EVENT_SAW_SUICUNE_AT_[LOCATION]

### Tin Tower Entrance — Kimono Girls (Crystal)
- Trigger: Clear Bell + all 8 badges
- What happens: Must battle 5 Kimono Girls again at Tin Tower entrance
- After winning: Suicune appears at Tin Tower base (Lv40, static encounter)

### Tin Tower Roof — Ho-Oh (Crystal)
- Trigger: Rainbow Wing (from Pewter City old man after all 16 badges)
- Battle: Ho-Oh Lv60
- Moves: Sacred Fire, Sunny Day, Fire Blast, Swift

unstructured_notes: Key QA concern: the Tin Tower Suicune encounter has two requirements — Clear Bell AND having 'seen' all three beasts (via Pokedex). The game checks a bitmask of Suicune encounter flags, not just the final one. Also note that Ho-Oh's moves in the story_events document list 'Sacred Fire, Sunny Day, Fire Blast, Swift' while the special_encounters document lists 'Sacred Fire, Safeguard, Gust, Recover' — the pokecrystal ASM must be checked as the authoritative source (the all_trainers.md or special_encounters.md moves table should reference the actual disassembly data). Always defer to the .asm file. The roaming-on-Fly rule is important for speedrunning strategies and must be faithfully implemented.

goal: Implement gift Pokemon and in-game trades — Togepi egg, Eevee, starter trade-backs, Shuckie, Kenya, all NPC trades
abstract_tasks: Implement Togepi Egg gift: Elm's aide in Violet City Pokemon Center gives egg after Zephyr Badge (EVENT_ZEPHYRBADGE set), egg hatches to Lv5 Togepi
Implement Eevee gift: Bill's house in Goldenrod City (after meeting Bill in Ecruteak Burned Tower), Lv20 Eevee, one per save file
Implement Shuckle (Shuckie) gift: Kirk/Mania's house in Cianwood, Lv15 Shuckle holding Berry, OT is Kirk, NPC may request it back later (player can refuse to keep it)
Implement Kenya gift: Guard in Route 35 gate gives Lv10 Spearow holding Mail, OT is Webster, deliver mail to man on Route 31 or keep the Spearow
Implement Tyrogue gift: Blackbelt Kiyo in Mt. Mortar inner chamber, given after defeating him, Lv10 Tyrogue

Implement all 7 NPC in-game trades from npc_trades.asm data: Abra->Machop (MUSCLE/MIKE/Goldenrod 5F), Bellsprout->Onix (ROCKY/KYLE/Violet), Krabby->Voltorb (VOLTY/TIM/Olivine), Haunter->Xatu (PAUL/CHRIS/Cianwood), Chansey->Aerodactyl (AEROY/KIM/Route 14), Dugtrio->Magneton (MAGGIE/FOREST/Power Plant), and female-only Dragonair->Dodrio (DORIS/EMY/Blackthorn)
Implement Dratini gift from Dragon's Den elder: Lv15 Dratini after passing Dragon Shrine quiz; if all 5 answers correct, Dratini knows ExtremeSpeed

source_data.special_encounters.md:
## Gift Pokemon
### Togepi Egg
Location: Violet City Pokemon Center
Given by: Mystery Egg from Mr. Pokemon (Route 30), hatched by Professor Elm's aide
Level: 5 (when hatched)

### Eevee
Location: Goldenrod City (Bill's house, after meeting him in Ecruteak)
Level: 20
Note: Only one per save file. Choose evolution wisely.

### Shuckle (Mania)
Location: Cianwood City (Kirk/Mania's house)
Level: 15
Held Item: Berry
Note: He asks you to take care of it. He may ask for it back later — say no to keep it.

### Spearow (Kenya)
Location: Route 35 gate
Given by: Guard in the gate
Level: 10
Held Item: Mail (letter to deliver to Route 31)
OT: Webster
Note: Deliver the mail to the guy on Route 31, or keep Kenya.

### Tyrogue
Location: Mt. Mortar (deep interior)
Given by: Blackbelt Kiyo (after defeating him)
Level: 10

### Dratini
Location: Dragon's Den (after answering Elder's quiz correctly)
Level: 15
Special: If you answer ALL questions correctly, the Dratini knows ExtremeSpeed

## In-Game Trades
| You Give | You Receive | Nickname | Held Item | OT Name | Location |
|----------|------------|----------|-----------|---------|----------|
| Abra | Machop | MUSCLE | Gold Berry | MIKE | Goldenrod Dept Store 5F |
| Bellsprout | Onix | ROCKY | Bitter Berry | KYLE | Violet City (house near gym) |
| Krabby | Voltorb | VOLTY | PrzCureBerry | TIM | Olivine City |
| Dragonair | Dodrio | DORIS | Smoke Ball | EMY | Blackthorn City (female player only) |
| Haunter | Xatu | PAUL | MysteryBerry | CHRIS | Cianwood City |
| Chansey | Aerodactyl | AEROY | Gold Berry | KIM | Route 14 |
| Dugtrio | Magneton | MAGGIE | Metal Coat | FOREST | Power Plant |

Notes:
- Trade #4 (Dragonair -> Dodrio) requires a female player character
- Traded Pokemon gain boosted EXP (1.5x)
- All traded Pokemon have preset DVs and OT IDs

unstructured_notes: In-game trades use the NPC trade engine which requires a matching species in the player's party. The traded-away Pokemon is removed, and the received Pokemon is generated with preset DVs and OT data from npc_trades.asm. All received trade Pokemon have preset happiness (usually 70) and level matching what is defined in the trade data. The Haunter->Xatu trade does NOT trigger Haunter's trade evolution to Gengar because the received Pokemon is Xatu (the given Haunter becomes an NPC's Pokemon and is not returned). The Kenya sidequest involves a scripted mail item attached to the Spearow; removing the mail from the Spearow before delivering it breaks the quest but lets the player keep Kenya. The female-only Dragonair trade checks the player character gender flag stored in save data.

goal: Implement hidden items — all 84 hidden items with coordinates, Itemfinder interaction, respawn rules
abstract_tasks: Implement hidden item data structures: per-map list of hidden item (x, y, item_id, event_flag) tuples loaded from pokecrystal hidden_items_complete.md
Implement Itemfinder mechanic: play direction-indicating beeps when player is adjacent to (within 1-2 tiles) a hidden item, beep intensity varies by distance
Implement hidden item pickup: pressing A on the correct tile with the correct event flag unset gives the item and sets the flag permanently (most hidden items are one-time)
Implement respawning hidden items: certain hidden items (notably Moon Stone at Mt. Moon on Monday nights) use time-based flags rather than permanent event flags
Verify all 84 hidden item coordinates match pokecrystal data exactly — coordinates are map-local (x, y) not world coordinates
Implement special hidden item interaction: Amulet Coin in Goldenrod Underground, Leftovers in Celadon Restaurant trash can, Soft Sand on Route 34 beach, each with correct event flag

source_data.item_locations.md:
## Notable Held Item Locations
- Leftovers: Celadon City Restaurant (trash can), S.S. Aqua
- Quick Claw: National Park (given by woman)
- Focus Band: Cianwood City (Shuckle man)
- Scope Lens: Goldenrod City (after Radio Tower event)
- BrightPowder: Defeat Red at Mt. Silver
- King's Rock: Slowpoke Well B2F
- Amulet Coin: Goldenrod Underground (Basement)
- Cleanse Tag: Tin Tower 5F
- Smoke Ball: Burned Tower B1F
- Charcoal: Azalea Town Charcoal Kiln (gift)
- Mystic Water: Cherrygrove City (Mr. Pokemon's house, Crystal)
- Miracle Seed: Route 32 (from the guy on the bridge)
- Sharp Beak: Route 40 (trainers, hidden)
- Soft Sand: Route 34 (hidden, beach area)
- Magnet: Olivine City Lighthouse 5F (hidden)
- NeverMeltIce: Ice Path B2F

## Key Item Locations
- Itemfinder: Ecruteak City (house next to Gym)
- Red Scale: Lake of Rage (after Red Gyarados event)
- Moon Stone: Ruins of Alph, Mt. Moon (Monday night), Mom shopping

## Berry Trees (Daily)
Berries regrow daily after being picked.
- Route 29: Berry
- Route 30: Berry
- Route 38: Berry
- Route 46: Berry

unstructured_notes: The Itemfinder in pokecrystal is a key item that when activated scans nearby tiles for hidden items using a proximity check. The beep pattern changes based on closeness. Hidden items in pokecrystal are stored in the map's object data as ITEM objects with hidden=TRUE. The event flag for each hidden item is globally unique. Most hidden items are one-time: once picked up the event flag is set and the item does not reappear. Some items with daily/weekly respawn (like the Moon Stone at Mt. Moon Square on Monday nights, and berry trees) use the ENGINE_DAILY flags or RTC-based checks rather than permanent event flags. The Leftovers in the Celadon Restaurant trash can is one of the most valuable hidden items in the game due to its held item effect and should be verified against pokecrystal map data for exact coordinates.

## Key Item Locations
- Itemfinder: Ecruteak City (house next to Gym)

## Notable Held Item Locations
- Leftovers: Celadon City Restaurant (trash can), S.S. Aqua
- Amulet Coin: Goldenrod Underground (Basement)
- Cleanse Tag: Tin Tower 5F
- Smoke Ball: Burned Tower B1F
- Soft Sand: Route 34 (hidden, beach area)

unstructured_notes: Key QA scenario: the Haunter->Xatu trade should NOT trigger Gengar evolution even though Haunter normally evolves via trade. The NPC trade engine gives a pre-generated Pokemon (Xatu) rather than evolving the traded-in Pokemon, so no evolution check is needed for the received Pokemon. For hidden items, the QA should confirm that items behind locked event gates (e.g., Cleanse Tag at Tin Tower 5F which requires ascending the tower) are not accessible before the relevant event flags. Also confirm that the Odd Egg at the Pokemon Communication Center (Crystal exclusive) generates a random species from the 7 possible baby Pokemon and applies the 14% shiny chance — the species selection uses RNG at hatch time, not at gift time.

goal: Implement phone rematch system — gym leader rematches, phone registration, day-of-week schedules, level scaling

Implement incoming call logic: CheckReceiveCallTimer, 50% random chance, map phone service check, ChooseRandomCaller from available registered contacts filtered by time-of-day mask
Implement call timing cycle: 20-minute delay after registration, then 10/5/3 minute cycles (wTimeCyclesSinceLastCall counter)

Implement all 23 phone rematch trainers: initial teams, rematch teams, and any gift items they call to offer (e.g., Lass Dana gives Thunderstone, Picnicker Liz gives Thunderstone, Bird Keeper Jose gives Star Piece)

Location: Route 30

Rematch Team: Lv30 Raticate

Location: Route 31
Initial Team: Lv2 Caterpie x3, Lv4 Caterpie
Rematch Team: Lv24 Butterfree x3, Lv28 Beedrill
Gift Items: Berry, PsnCureBerry, Bitter Berry

Location: Route 32

Location: Route 38

## Swarm Notifications

## Gym Leader Phone Numbers
In Pokemon Crystal, gym leaders do NOT offer rematches.
- Falkner: After getting all 8 badges, talk to him in Violet Gym (Saturday morning)
- Bugsy: After getting all 8 badges, talk to him in Azalea Gym (Thursday)
- Whitney: After getting all 8 badges, talk to her in Goldenrod Dept Store 6F (Saturday afternoon)
- Morty: After getting all 8 badges, talk to him in Burned Tower 1F (Tuesday night)
- Chuck: After getting all 8 badges, talk to his wife outside Cianwood Gym (Wednesday night)
- Jasmine: After getting all 8 badges, talk to her in Olivine Cafe (every day)
- Pryce: After getting all 8 badges, talk to him near Lake of Rage (every morning)
- Clair: After getting all 8 badges, talk to her in Dragon's Den (every day)
source_data.phone_engine.md:
## Phone Contact Struct (PHONE_CONTACT_SIZE bytes)
| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_CLASS | Trainer class (0 for non-trainer) |
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_ID | Trainer ID within class |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_BANK | Bank for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_ADDR | Address for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_TIME | Time-of-day mask for player calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_BANK | Bank for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_ADDR | Address for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_TIME | Time-of-day mask for incoming calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_GROUP | Map group where contact resides |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_NUMBER | Map number where contact resides |

## Call Timing
- Cycle 0: 20 minutes
- Cycle 1: 10 minutes
- Cycle 2: 5 minutes
- Cycle 3+: 3 minutes

unstructured_notes: Crystal's gym leaders explicitly do NOT offer rematches unlike HeartGold/SoulSilver. Their phone numbers are collectible and their calls only provide tips and rare Pokemon sighting hints. Each gym leader's number is obtainable at a specific location and time-of-day, and the schedule must be implemented faithfully. The phone system's outgoing call vs incoming call scripts are distinct: SCRIPT1 is player-initiated, SCRIPT2 is NPC-initiated. Swarm events triggered by phone calls set a temporary swarm flag that changes which Pokemon appear in the specified route/area's encounter table for the remainder of that in-game day.

goal: Implement item locations — all obtainable items by location, shop inventories, department store, special shops (underground, Celadon)
abstract_tasks: Implement all Poke Mart inventories for every city/route (23 distinct mart inventories): Cherrygrove, Violet, Azalea, Cianwood, Goldenrod 2F/3F/4F/5F, Olivine, Ecruteak, Mahogany pre/post-Rocket, Blackthorn, Viridian, Pewter, Cerulean, Lavender, Vermilion, Celadon 2F-5F, Fuchsia, Saffron, Indigo Plateau

Implement special shop inventories: Goldenrod Underground Bargain Shop (Nugget/Pearl/Big Pearl/Stardust/Star Piece at discounted prices, daily stock), Buena's Prize Exchange (Ultra Ball/Full Restore/Nugget/Rare Candy/vitamins for points)
Implement Goldenrod Department Store Rooftop Sale: two sale configurations that rotate, each with discounted items

Implement mart unlock conditions: Cherrygrove post-Pokedex inventory upgrade, Mahogany fake shop (TinyMushroom/Slowpoketail) before Rockets cleared vs standard inventory after, Goldenrod 5F trade counter variants based on player gender and game state

Implement evolution stone locations: Fire Stone from Bill's Grandfather (show Vulpix), Water Stone (show Staryu), Thunderstone (show Pichu), Leaf Stone (show Oddish); phone gift stones from Dana (Thunderstone), Liz (Thunderstone), Alan (Fire Stone), Gina (Leaf Stone), Tully (Water Stone)

Implement key item acquisition events: Bicycle from Goldenrod Bike Shop (free), Itemfinder from Ecruteak house, Old Rod from Route 32 Fisherman, Good Rod from Olivine Fisherman, Super Rod from Route 12 Kanto house, Coin Case from Underground trade

### Indigo Plateau

## Key Item Locations
- Town Map: Given by rival's mom (New Bark Town)
- Bicycle: Bicycle Shop in Goldenrod City (free)
- Itemfinder: Ecruteak City (house next to Gym)
- Old Rod: Route 32 (Fisherman on the bridge)
- Good Rod: Olivine City (Fisherman in house south of Pokemon Center)
- Super Rod: Route 12 (house on fishing peninsula, Kanto)
- Coin Case: Underground in Goldenrod City
- Exp. Share: Mr. Pokemon's house (trade Red Scale)
- Clear Bell: Radio Tower (after Team Rocket event, Crystal only)
- Silver Wing: Radio Tower Director (after Team Rocket event)
- Rainbow Wing: Radio Tower Director (Crystal only)
- Basement Key: Team Rocket HQ in Mahogany Town
- Card Key: Underground Warehouse (Goldenrod Radio Tower event)
- Machine Part: Cerulean Gym (from Team Rocket Grunt)
- Lost Item: Vermilion City Pokemon Fan Club
- Pass: Copycat in Saffron City (trade Lost Item)

## Special Shop Inventories
### Bargain Shop (Goldenrod Underground, daily)
- Nugget 4,500 | Pearl 650 | Big Pearl 3,500 | Stardust 900 | Star Piece 4,600
### Buena's Prize Exchange
- Ultra Ball 2pts | Full Restore 2pts | Nugget 3pts | Rare Candy 3pts | vitamins 5pts each
### Rooftop Sale
Sale 1: Poke Ball 150, Great Ball 500, Super Potion 500, Full Heal 500, Revive 1200
Sale 2: Hyper Potion 1000, Full Restore 2000, Full Heal 500, Ultra Ball 1000, Protein 7800

unstructured_notes: The Goldenrod Department Store 5F has multiple sub-inventories that depend on player state — early game only has TM Thunderpunch/Fire Punch/Ice Punch (Goldenrod5F1), while later states add TM Headbutt (5F2) and TM Rock Smash (5F3/5F4). These inventory transitions are tied to EVENT flags from Ilex Forest (HM01 Cut = TM Headbutt becomes available) and Rock Smash events. The Mahogany fake shop selling Slowpoketail is intentional game design (satirizing Team Rocket's Slowpoke tail crime ring). The Bargain Shop's daily stock is the same items every day — the 'daily' refers to a full-price refresh, not different items. The Rooftop Sale is a timed event that occurs on certain weekends.

Verify Buena's Prize Exchange point costs match pokecrystal data: Ultra Ball 2pts, Full Restore 2pts, Nugget 3pts, Rare Candy 3pts, vitamins 5pts
Run headless simulation of Rooftop Sale availability: confirm sale items and discounted prices for both Sale 1 and Sale 2 configurations
Verify key item acquisition events trigger correctly: Bicycle given free at Goldenrod Bike Shop, Super Rod only available from Route 12 house in Kanto (post-Elite Four area)

unstructured_notes: A critical QA point: in Crystal, gym leaders cannot be rematched via phone. This is a known difference from later remakes. If the implementation accidentally allows gym leader rematches, it would be a significant deviation from the original. The phone system should only offer rematches for the 23 route trainers. Also verify that swarm notifications actually modify the encounter table for the relevant route (e.g., Dunsparce appears in Dark Cave's encounter table when Hiker Anthony's swarm call is active). The Rooftop Sale event timing should be verified against pokecrystal's rooftop sale script — it's a limited-time event tied to specific in-game days.

goal: Implement item use mechanics — healing items (Potion/Super/Hyper/Max/Full Restore), status cure items, Revive, Sacred Ash
abstract_tasks: Implement RestoreHPEffect dispatch: Potion (+20), Super Potion (+50), Hyper Potion (+200), Max Potion (full HP), Fresh Water (+50), Soda Pop (+60), Lemonade (+80), Moomoo Milk (+100), RageCandyBar (+20), Berry (+10), Gold Berry (+30), Berry Juice (+20)
Implement HP item validation checks: target Pokemon must not be fainted (HP > 0) and must not be at full HP; display 'It won't have any effect' if either condition fails
Implement FullRestoreEffect: restore to full HP AND clear all non-volatile status (sleep/freeze/para/burn/poison), cannot be used on fainted Pokemon

Implement StatusHealingEffect dispatch: Antidote (poison only), Burn Heal (burn only), Ice Heal (freeze only), Awakening (sleep only), Parlyz Heal (paralysis only), Full Heal (any status); each must validate that the target has the matching status condition before use

Implement ReviveEffect: Revive restores fainted Pokemon to 50% max HP (rounded down), Max Revive restores to full HP, Revival Herb restores to full HP with -happiness penalty; target must be fainted (HP == 0)
Implement SacredAshEffect: revives ALL fainted party Pokemon to full HP in a single use; requires at least one fainted Pokemon in party; consumed on use
Implement bitter medicine happiness penalties: EnergyPowder -5 (friendship < 200) or -10 (friendship >= 200), Energy Root -10 or -15, Heal Powder -5, Revival Herb -10 or -15

| Full Restore | Full HP + cure all status |
| Fresh Water | 50 |
| Soda Pop | 60 |
| Lemonade | 80 |
| Moomoo Milk | 100 |
| RageCandyBar | 20 |
| Berry | 10 |
| Gold Berry | 30 |
| Berry Juice | 20 |
| Energy Root | 200 (happiness penalty) |
| Energypowder | 50 (happiness penalty) |

## Status Healing Items
| Item | Cures |
|------|-------|
| Antidote | Poison |
| Burn Heal | Burn |
| Ice Heal | Freeze |
| Awakening | Sleep |
| Parlyz Heal | Paralysis |
| Full Heal | All status conditions |
| Full Restore | All status + full HP |
| PsnCureBerry | Poison (auto-use in battle as held item) |
| PrzCureBerry | Paralysis (auto-use) |
| Burnt Berry | Freeze (auto-use) |
| Ice Berry | Burn (auto-use) -- NOTE: counterintuitive name |
| Mint Berry | Sleep (auto-use) |
| MiracleBerry | Any status (auto-use) |
| Bitter Berry | Confusion |

## Revive Items
| Item | Effect |
|------|--------|
| Revive | Restores a fainted Pokemon to 50% max HP |
| Max Revive | Restores a fainted Pokemon to 100% max HP |
| Revival Herb | Restores a fainted Pokemon to full HP (happiness penalty) |
| Sacred Ash | Revives ALL fainted party Pokemon to full HP |

## Item Use Validation
Items check various conditions before use:
- HP healing items: Pokemon must not be at full HP and must not be fainted
- Status heals: Pokemon must have the relevant status condition
- Revives: Pokemon must be fainted (HP = 0)

## Status Healing Items
From data/items/heal_status.asm:
| Item | Cures | Context |
|------|-------|--------|
| Antidote | Poison | Menu use |
| Burn Heal | Burn | Menu use |
| Ice Heal | Freeze | Menu use |
| Awakening | Sleep | Menu use |
| Parlyz Heal | Paralysis | Menu use |
| Full Heal | All status | Menu use |
| Full Restore | All status | Menu use |
| Heal Powder | All status | Menu use (bitter) |

unstructured_notes: Item use in and out of battle uses the same effect functions but the context differs: in battle, the animation plays and the turn is consumed; in the overworld menu, the item is applied directly. HP healing items cap at max HP without error — applying a Potion to a Pokemon at max HP - 1 gives exactly 1 HP, not 20 (capped at max). Sacred Ash is extremely rare (only found at Tin Tower and from some trades) and its effect on ALL fainted Pokemon is unique. The Ice Berry (confusingly named) actually cures Burn — not Freeze. The Burnt Berry cures Freeze. These counterintuitive names must be implemented exactly as in the source data. Bitter medicine items apply happiness penalties immediately on use from the menu.

goal: Implement battle items — X items (Attack, Defend, Speed, Special, Accuracy), Guard Spec, Dire Hit, stat stage application
abstract_tasks: Implement XItemEffect: X Attack, X Defend, X Speed, X Special each apply +1 to the corresponding battle stat stage using the same stat modification routines as Swords Dance/Growl etc.; must validate in-battle usage only
Implement XAccuracyEffect: X Accuracy applies +1 to accuracy stage in battle, separate from the 4 XItemEffect items because accuracy is not a primary stat
Implement DireHitEffect: Dire Hit raises the critical hit stage by +1 in battle, analogous to Focus Energy but via item; must not stack with Focus Energy beyond the crit stage cap

Implement GuardSpecEffect: Guard Spec prevents stat reduction for the user's side for 5 turns (MIST_COUNT), must interact correctly with Safeguard and prevent both enemy moves and self-inflicted stat drops (from Psych Up, Belly Drum, etc.) — note: Guard Spec blocks REDUCTION only, not raising
Implement battle item validation: all X items, Guard Spec, Dire Hit must fail gracefully if used outside battle with message 'This is not the right place to use this item'
Implement AI trainer item usage for X items: trainers can use X items from their item slots, AI scoring should favor using offensive X items when at a strategic advantage

## Battle Items
| Item | Price | Effect |
|------|------:|--------|
| X ACCURACY | 950 | Raises Accuracy in battle |
| GUARD SPEC. | 700 | Prevents stat lowering for 5 turns |
| DIRE HIT | 650 | Raises critical hit ratio in battle |

unstructured_notes: Guard Spec's 5-turn mist effect is stored in wMistCount for the player's side. Unlike Safeguard (which blocks status conditions), Guard Spec blocks STAT REDUCTION only — it does not block status moves that do not reduce stats. The interactions are: Guard Spec blocks opponent using Growl/Leer/etc., but does not block Toxic, Thunder Wave, or Confuse Ray. This is a key distinction from Safeguard. X Special only boosts Special Attack in Gen 2 (not Sp. Def as well, since they split). The player can use X items against their own Pokemon in any order during the battle turn, and the item use consumes their turn just like selecting a move. Items used by trainers (AI) come from their pre-defined item slots, not the player's bag.

goal: QA audit of sprints 169-170 — verify healing values, status cure targeting, X item stat stages, Guard Spec duration
abstract_tasks: Verify exact HP healing values against item_details.md heal_hp.asm table: Potion=20, Super Potion=50, Hyper Potion=200, Fresh Water=50, Soda Pop=60, Lemonade=80, Moomoo Milk=100, RageCandyBar=20, Berry=10, Gold Berry=30
Verify HP item caps at max HP: apply Potion to a Pokemon at max HP-1 and confirm exactly 1 HP is restored (not 20), apply to full HP Pokemon and confirm failure with appropriate message

Verify status cure targeting: Antidote only works when target has poison status (regular or toxic), Burn Heal only when burned, Ice Heal only when frozen, Awakening only when asleep, Parlyz Heal only when paralyzed; confirm failure message for wrong status
Verify counterintuitive berry names: Ice Berry cures Burn (not Freeze), Burnt Berry cures Freeze (not Burn); these must match pokecrystal data/items/heal_status.asm exactly
Verify Sacred Ash revives all fainted party members to full HP in one use and is consumed; confirm it fails gracefully if no party members are fainted

Verify X item effects apply +1 stat stage using identical code path to Swords Dance; verify Guard Spec lasts exactly 5 turns and blocks stat reduction but not status conditions like Toxic or Thunder Wave
Verify Dire Hit grants +1 crit stage and does not stack with Focus Energy beyond the game's crit stage cap (stage 4 = 1/2 crit rate maximum)

source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 14: Focus Energy + Critical Hit Interaction
Setup: Pokemon uses Focus Energy.
Outcome: In Gen 2, Focus Energy correctly adds +1 to the critical hit stage. With Focus Energy and a high-crit move (like Slash), the Pokemon reaches stage 3 (1/3 crit rate). Maximum stage 4+ gives 1/2 crit rate.
ASM: BattleCommand_Critical checks SUBSTATUS_FOCUS_ENERGY and adds 1 to the crit stage if set.

unstructured_notes: The QA should specifically test the counterintuitive berry-status pairings against the source ASM. The heal_status.asm file is the definitive reference: Ice Berry heals Burn, Burnt Berry heals Freeze. These names were likely swapped intentionally or accidentally in the original game and must be faithfully replicated. Guard Spec's 5-turn duration uses wMistCount — the QA should confirm the counter decrements at end-of-turn and the effect expires exactly after 5 turns (turn of use counts as turn 1, expires at the start of turn 6). Also confirm Guard Spec does NOT prevent Toxic or Thunder Wave (status conditions), only stat reduction moves like Growl, Screech, Charm, Cotton Spore, etc.
